title: Dense Spicy Australian Potato Pancakes
description: Fluffy, spicy pancakes made with grated potatoes, kombu cold extract and a tangy sour cream drizzle, a nostalgic Australian comfort food.
instructions: In a large bowl mix grated potatoes, flour, salt, pepper, chili flakes, and kombu cold extract until just combined.
Fold in sour cream until a sticky batter forms, adding a little water if too thick.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat; once shimmering, pour ¼ cup batter and spread into a flat circle.
Cook 4 minutes until edges set, flip and cook another 3 minutes until golden and puffed.
Repeat with remaining batter, adding oil as needed.
While pancakes cook, sauté minced garlic in 1 tsp oil until fragrant, then toss with sliced green onions.
Plate pancakes and drizzle with the garlic green onion mixture; serve hot with a dollop of sour cream.
Optional: sprinkle extra chili flakes for extra heat.
